medications.indication_rationale: Oral antibiotic for extensive/facial impetigo; confirm a weight-appropriate paediatric dose.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paediatric weight-based dosing (source note lists 250 mg four times daily) — confirm for weight

medications.indication_rationale: Apply to lesions after gently soaking off crusts.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply three times daily to lesions (as per source note)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Gently soak off crusts with a warm damp cloth before the ointment; strict hand hygiene, separate towels/linen, keep nails short, and keep the child home from childcare until 24h of antibiotics/lesions dry; treat affected contacts.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Improves treatment penetration and prevents transmission/autoinoculation.

patient_education_points.point: This is impetigo — a very contagious skin infection. The oral and cream antibiotics clear it; soaking off the crusts first lets the cream work.

Keep the child home from childcare until they've had 24 hours of antibiotics or the sores are dry, use separate towels, wash hands well, and keep nails short. Treat any others in the house who get it.
If the redness spreads across the cheeks toward the eyes, the child develops a high fever, becomes very sleepy, or refuses all fluids — go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Cessation of crusting/weeping and absence of cellulitis.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spread toward the eyes, high fever, lethargy or refusing fluids — emergency care.

must_include_items: Diagnose impetigo and prescribe oral + topical antibiotics (weight-appropriate) for extensive facial disease
Screen for facial/periorbital cellulitis and HSV
Strict hygiene, exclusion from childcare, and contact treatment
acceptable_alternatives: Topical antibiotic alone only if truly localised (not this extensive case)
errors_of_commission: Invented paediatric antibiotic dose without weight verification
Treating extensive facial impetigo with topical only
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires oral + topical antibiotics for extensive facial impetigo, hygiene/exclusion/contact advice, and a facial-cellulitis safety-net; paediatric dosing must be clinician-verified.
